channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: UN report reveals alarming extent of Iran’s increased surveillance of women
item.description: Iran increasingly relies on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country’s mandatory headscarf in public, even as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Got a road toll text? Don’t click on this ‘smishing’ scam
item.description: State officials are warning Americans not to respond to a surge of scam road toll collection texts. The texts impersonating state road toll collection agencies attempt to get phone users to reveal financial information, such as credit or debit cards or bank accounts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Android 16 Beta 3 has arrived – here are the 4 features I think will be the most useful
item.description: Android 16 Beta 3 has arrived, bringing with it a handful of useful new features that aim to improve accessibility, make it easier to monitor the longevity of your phone, and keep local networks secure.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Commerce secretary says Trump wants TikTok deal done under current deadline
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Friday said President Trump would like to hammer out a deal on TikTok's future ownership under the current deadline, saying the president "doesn't like to ask for extensions." "So, it's in Donald Trump's hands.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: US government warns Medusa ransomware has hit hundreds of critical infrastructure targets
item.description: Medusa ransomware has been around since 2021, targeting more than 300 critical infrastructure firms, CISA warns.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Women in tech statistics: The hard truths of an uphill battle
item.description: Diversity is critical to IT performance. Diverse teams perform better, hire better talent, have more engaged members, and retain workers better than those that don’t focus on diversity and inclusion, according to a recent report from McKinsey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel under Tan: What enterprise IT buyers need to know
item.description: Intel’s appointment of semiconductor veteran Lip-Bu Tan as CEO marks a critical moment for the company and its enterprise customers. With rising competition from AMD, Arm-based chips, and RISC-V alternatives, Intel faces mounting pressure to defend its x86 dominance.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Java hiring plans slip, survey says
item.description: Slightly more than half of Java shops surveyed (51%) have plans to add more Java developers in the coming year, down from 60% in 2024, according to a report by Perforce, a provider of Java development and devops tools.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel taps industry veteran as newest CEO in latest comeback attempt
item.description: Struggling chipmaker Intel has hired former board member and semiconductor industry veteran Lip-Bu Tan as the latest in a succession of CEOs to attempt to turn around a once-dominant company that helped define Silicon Valley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: SAP patches severe vulnerabilities in NetWeaver and Commerce apps
item.description: SAP has patched high-severity vulnerabilities in its Commerce and NetWeaver enterprise software packages. The updates came as part of 25 security patches released on Tuesday for the latest edition of SAP’s monthly patch release cycle.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Millions of Americans are missing out on cheap unlimited cloud storage - how to check if you are one of them
item.description: Millions of Americans can take advantage of unlimited cloud storage services with these two leading carriers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump calls EU ‘nasty’ while sitting alongside NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called the European Union "nasty" while sitting alongside NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte. Trump complained about the steep tariff on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Mozilla: Update Firefox immediately
item.description: Expired certificates have recently caused a lot of chaos, including for Chromecast users. With that in mind, Mozilla is now urging all Firefox users to immediately update the browser to the latest version.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Forget AI – WhatsApp is planning a simple messages feature that could be its most useful upgrade in years
item.description: A WhatsApp beta contains a threads feature that looks set to make keeping track of topics in chats a lot easier, and I can't wait to try it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Watchdog Group Seeks Investigation into FAA's Business with Elon Musk's Starlink
item.description: A watchdog group has asked the Transportation Department's inspector general to investigate the Federal Aviation Administration's business with Elon Musk's Starlink, citing potential conflict of interest violations.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SUSE expands AI tools to control workloads, LLM usage
item.description: SUSE expanded its AI platform today with new tools and a new partnership but SUSE AI, which first launched in November of 2024, lags far behind other AI platforms.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How AI-enabled ‘bossware’ is being used to track and evaluate your work
item.description: Employee monitoring software, also called “bossware” and “tattleware,” is increasingly being used to track and manage employees remotely via a business network or by using desktop software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability
item.description: This approach emphasizes the importance of integrating security measures into the software development lifecycle from the beginning. The post Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Arista delivers intelligent load balancing, AI job-centric observability
item.description: Arista Networks has added load balancing and AI job-centric observability features to core software products in an effort to help enterprise customers grow and effectively manage AI networked environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI takes on rivals with new Responses API, Agents SDK
item.description: OpenAI’s new Responses API and an upgraded Agents SDK will help enterprises more easily build agents with advanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities, the company said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Building generative AI? Get ready for generative UI
item.description: There’s currently no stopping generative AI spending. Analyst firm Omdia expects spending on generative AI applications to hit $58 billion by 2028. Yet while this level of investment is a significant amount, actual deployment of generative AI applications is still slow.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: At the tactical edge, Army wants 58 zero trust capabilities
item.description: CWO Four Ben Koontz, the director of the Army’s functional management office for zero trust, said a recent pilot successfully tested these cyber tools. The post At the tactical edge, Army wants 58 zero trust capabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
